violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Establishments RICFSM has left and a new lead has been hired. Establishment is to apply for a RICFSM with RIDOH. Application was printed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 02/11/2026

Side C door 9 to the outside, located in the hallway, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/11/2026
The hand sink in the employee bathroom was observed leaking. Establishment is to fix leak. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026

The back right corner in the dry storage area and corners of the cafeteria have an accumulation of soil residue, droppings, and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Establishment is to clean any and all droppings using appropriate means of sanitization. New Violation. Correct By: 02/04/2026

The door to the outside, located in the stairway hall (side C door 9), is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 10/15/2025

Behind the cabinet and vending machines in the cafeteria area has an accumulation of soil residue, droppings, and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Establishment is to clean any and all droppings using appropriate means of sanitization. New Violation. Correct By: 10/08/2025

2 door reach-in #2 was observed held at 47 degrees Fahrenheit. Most food in unit was observed between 42-44 DF. All food between 42-44 DF was moved to properly functioning refrigeration. Cheese sauce was observed at 49 DF and was voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Establishment is not to store any TCS foods in unit until unit it able to hold an ambient temperature of 41 DF or less. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/22/2025

The presence of pests is not controlled as evidenced by droppings in the dry storage area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment is to work with a licensed pest control company to eliminate pests. Establishment is to increase pest control. Establishment is to clean any and all droppings with appropriate means of sanitizing and have pest control report available for inspector at the time of next inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/22/2025

The door to the outside, located in the stairway hall, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 09/22/2025
Shredded cheese was observed with blue debris. PIC discarded. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2025

Shredded cheese was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. PIC discarded. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2025

The presence of rodents are not controlled as evidenced by droppings. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2025

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the back storage area. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2025

Food prep sink in the kitchen area is lacking an air gap. A plumbing system shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply by providing an air gap or installing an approved backflow prevention device. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2024

The sink used for food preparation is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2024
Rodent droppings observed in the storage room and corners of kitchen. Areas to be cleaned and monitored. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2024

The sink used for food preparation is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2024

The threshold to the side door (rear hallway) and the floor (serving line) are in poor repair. Openings observed. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/06/2024
Inspector observed unused equipment (slicer) and missing floor tiles (hot water tank/storage room). Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2024

The door to the outside, located in the cafeteria, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/28/2023
Milk Cooler door gaskets were observed torn and in poor repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/28/2023
Inspector observed leak coming from hot water tank in storage area. Plumbing system must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/28/2023
Inspector observed a hole in the wall located in the kitchen area. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/28/2023

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2023

The door to the outside, located in the cafeteria, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2023

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings throughout the kitchen and back storage area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2023
Ready to eat food (apple)s were not individual wrapped to prevent contamination.PIC corrected with use of tongs. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2023

Refrigerator was observed in poor repair. Ambient temperature of 2 door refrigerator was 46-50 degrees Fahrenheit.PIC removed all TCS foods into a working refrigerator. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2023
